Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demands For Production And Notice Of Hearing
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MEGAN KLEIN, Esq. of BD&J, PC. (tel: 310-887-1818) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JONATHAN LOUIE, Esq. of Clapp, Moroney, Vucinich, Beeman & Scheley (tel: 650-989-5400) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF MATTHEW ROGIAN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demands For Production And Notice Of Hearing.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court grants in part and denies in part the motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents.

The court denies the request for further production as to request numbers 10, 11, 12, 13, and 16. Unagi's responses are in substantial compliance with its code obligations; Unagi has represented that it has no documents in its possession and Plaintiff offers no facts defeating that assertion. (That a lawsuit was filed in another jurisdiction is not to the contrary; Unagi does not necessarily have documents concerning that case and is not obliged to obtain documents from third parties who are not its agents to respond to the RFP.)

The court denies the request as to RFP 15 (asking for battery incidents): Unagi's separate statement and opposing papers state that additional documents have been produced and that no further documents are responsive.

The court grants the request as to the following RFPs: Unagi shall amend its response to RFP 14, asking for information about front wheel incidents, to state in its response that a reasonably diligent search was performed and no responsive documents were located, the straightforward representation it makes in its opposing separate statement (but not in its RFP response).

The court notes that Plaintiff's apparent position that Unagi must produce records in the possession of nonparties who are not its agents or employees is incorrect. Plaintiff's meet and confer efforts were sufficient.

Unagi shall provide further responses to the RFP as specified above within 15 days of notice of entry of this order. The court does not find that either party acted with substantial justification and does not award sanctions.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MEGAN KLEIN, Esq. of BD&J, PC. (tel: 310-887-1818) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JONATHAN LOUIE, Esq. of Clapp, Moroney, Vucinich, Beeman & Scheley (tel: 650-989-5400) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF MATTHEW ROGIAN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories Set One is GRANTED.

The interrogatories at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence in this action alleging, inter alia, product liability. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown that Defendant Unagi Inc.'s responses are incomplete. Specifically, in its responses, Unagi contended it had insufficient information to respond. In its opposing separate statement, Unagi has set forth additional unverified factual information about the scope of its search for information and what knowledge is in its possession, and has affirmatively stated that it does not have any responsive information. Plaintiff is entitled to receive that such an answer in verified responses. Unagi is required to provide information within its control, including information known to its agents and employees, such as court proceedings or complaints of which it is aware. Its initial responses did not unambiguously state that it was providing that responsive information.

Plaintiff's meet and confer efforts were sufficient.

Unagi shall provide further responses to Special Interrogatory Nos. 3 through 9 within 15 days of notice of entry of this order. The court does not find Unagi acted with substantial justification; accordingly, sanctions are mandatory. Defendant shall pay $860 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 15 days from notice of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
